prompt: Read the text "Jetlag" and write one word in each gap.
explanation: Verbs of motion (travel, go, fly) use to. "In the middle of the night" and "in the morning" use in. Clock-time-like points (midday, eleven pm, 3 pm, midnight) use at, as do specific buildings/points on a journey like an airport. Cities use in.
passageTemplate:
When you travel {{blank-1}} the other side of the world, jetlag is a real problem. You find yourself awake {{blank-2}} the middle of the night and you feel like going to bed {{blank-3}} the morning, just when everyone else is getting up.

Jetlag happens when you go {{blank-4}} a country where the time is very different. For example, you might leave London {{blank-5}} midday and fly {{blank-6}} Los Angeles. The flight takes about eleven hours, so when you arrive {{blank-7}} Los Angeles airport, your body thinks you're there {{blank-8}} eleven pm.

But Los Angeles is eight hours behind London, so you actually get there {{blank-9}} 3 pm local time. So, {{blank-10}} midnight Los Angeles time, your body, which still thinks it's {{blank-11}} London, says it's 8 am.

It takes a few days for your body clock to change.
